metadata.title: U.S. State-by-State Building Construction & Environmental Standards (2026)
metadata.description: Structured data for AI document builders and compliance tools. Covers primary building codes, energy standards, stormwater thresholds, wetlands programs, and key agencies per state. Use as reference; always verify with official sources as codes and rules change. Data compiled from ICC, EPA, state agencies, and public sources.
metadata.usage_note: For each state, AI should check the 'official_resources' links and confirm current editions/amendments before generating compliance documents. Stormwater is almost universally 1 acre unless noted. Wetlands require USACE + state coordination.

national_defaults.building_code_model: International Building Code (IBC) for commercial/public; International Residential Code (IRC) for 1-2 family dwellings
national_defaults.energy_code_model: International Energy Conservation Code (IECC) or ASHRAE 90.1
national_defaults.stormwater_threshold: 1 acre or more land disturbance (or part of larger common plan)
national_defaults.stormwater_agency_type: State environmental agency (DEQ, DEP, DNR, etc.) implementing NPDES
national_defaults.wetlands: USACE Section 404 + state wetlands/water quality programs (some states assume 404)
national_defaults.enforcement_note: Even in statewide code states, local jurisdictions often enforce and may add amendments.

building_code.authority: Alabama Building Commission / local jurisdictions

Alabama.notes: Home-rule elements; check local for amendments.

building_code.authority: Alaska Department of Labor and Workforce Development / local

Alaska.notes: Limited statewide code; many local adoptions vary.

building_code.authority: Arizona Department of Housing / local jurisdictions

wetlands.notes: Limited wetlands but strict where present

Arizona.notes: Strong local control; Phoenix and Tucson have robust programs.

building_code.authority: Arkansas Department of Labor and Licensing

stormwater.agency: Arkansas Department of Energy and Environment (ADEE)

building_code.authority: California Building Standards Commission
building_code.residential: California Residential Code (Title 24, based on 2021 IRC with major amendments)
building_code.commercial: California Building Code (Title 24, based on 2021 IBC with major amendments)
building_code.energy: 2022 or later Title 24 Energy Code (very stringent, exceeds IECC)
building_code.enforcement: Statewide base code with local amendments/enforcement

stormwater.threshold: 1 acre (or smaller in some MS4 areas)
stormwater.agency: State Water Resources Control Board (SWRCB) / Regional Water Boards
stormwater.notes: Strong post-construction LID and treatment requirements; coastal considerations

California.notes: One of the most stringent states. Title 24 energy code is mandatory and updated triennially. Check local for additional green building requirements.

building_code.authority: Colorado Division of Housing / local (home-rule heavy)
building_code.residential: 2021 IRC (local adoption required in many areas)
building_code.energy: 2024 IECC-based Model Low Energy and Carbon Code (adopted 2025, effective 2026 in many jurisdictions)
building_code.enforcement: Primarily local / home-rule with state model code push

stormwater.agency: Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment (CDPHE)

Colorado.notes: Strong push for advanced energy and electrification codes. Many cities (Denver, Boulder) have stretch codes.

building_code.authority: Connecticut Department of Administrative Services / Office of the State Building Inspector

stormwater.threshold: 1 acre (or 5,000 sq ft in some MS4 areas)
stormwater.agency: Connecticut Department of Energy and Environmental Protection (DEEP)
stormwater.notes: Strong LID and volume control standards

wetlands.notes: Inland wetlands regulated at local level too

Connecticut.notes: Active in adopting latest energy codes with electric-ready provisions.

building_code.authority: Delaware Office of State Fire Marshal / local

stormwater.agency: Delaware Department of Natural Resources and Environmental Control (DNREC)
stormwater.permit: Sediment and Stormwater Management Plan / NPDES

building_code.authority: Florida Building Commission
building_code.residential: Florida Residential Code (based on 2021 IRC with state amendments, updated triennially)
building_code.commercial: Florida Building Code (based on 2021 IBC with major state amendments)
building_code.energy: Florida Energy Conservation Code (based on IECC with Florida-specific provisions)
building_code.enforcement: Strong statewide code with local enforcement

stormwater.agency: Florida Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) + Water Management Districts
stormwater.permit: NPDES Construction General Permit + Environmental Resource Permit (ERP)
stormwater.notes: Hurricane/wind + stormwater integration; ERP often required

wetlands.agency: DEP + USACE Jacksonville District + Water Management Districts
wetlands.notes: Very strict wetlands and coastal protections

Florida.notes: One of the strongest statewide building code systems in the U.S., especially for wind resistance.

building_code.authority: Georgia Department of Community Affairs
Hawaii State Building Code Council / counties
building_code.residential: 2021 IRC with Hawaii amendments (wind, seismic, flood)
building_code.enforcement: State base with county enforcement (Honolulu, etc.)

stormwater.agency: Hawaii Department of Health (DOH) / Clean Water Branch
stormwater.notes: Tropical climate and coastal BMPs critical

wetlands.notes: Coastal wetlands and coral reef protections

Hawaii.notes: Strong seismic, wind, and flood provisions due to location.

building_code.authority: Idaho Division of Building Safety
Illinois Capital Development Board / local (home-rule cities like Chicago have own codes)
building_code.enforcement: State model code; local adoption/enforcement (Chicago strict)

wetlands.notes: Great Lakes and Mississippi River wetlands

Illinois.notes: Illinois adopted 2024 IECC in late 2025. Chicago has its own stringent code.

building_code.authority: Indiana Department of Homeland Security / Fire and Building Safety
Iowa Department of Public Safety / State Fire Marshal / local

wetlands.agency: Iowa DNR + USACE Rock Island District

Iowa.notes: Code update in progress; check current edition.

building_code.authority: Kansas Office of the State Fire Marshal / local (home-rule)

stormwater.agency: Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE)

building_code.authority: Kentucky Department of Housing, Buildings and Construction
Louisiana State Uniform Construction Code Council
Maine Department of Public Safety / Office of State Fire Marshal

Maine.notes: Northeast leader in energy code updates.

building_code.authority: Maryland Department of Labor / Division of Labor and Industry

stormwater.permit: NPDES Construction General Permit + Erosion & Sediment Control
stormwater.notes: Lower threshold common; Chesapeake Bay protections

Maryland.notes: Lower stormwater threshold and strong Bay protections are key features.

building_code.authority: Massachusetts Board of Building Regulations and Standards (BBRS)
building_code.enforcement: Statewide base code with local enforcement; stretch codes available

stormwater.notes: Strong volume control and LID standards

Massachusetts.notes: Stretch energy code is a model for other states.

building_code.authority: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA) / Bureau of Construction Codes

stormwater.agency: Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes, and Energy (EGLE)

wetlands.notes: Significant Great Lakes coastal and inland wetlands

building_code.authority: Minnesota Department of Labor and Industry

stormwater.notes: Strong volume control in sensitive areas

building_code.authority: Mississippi State Board of Contractors / local
Missouri Division of Fire Safety / local (home-rule heavy)

wetlands.agency: DNR + USACE Kansas City/St. Louis Districts

building_code.authority: Montana Department of Labor and Industry / Building Codes Bureau
Nebraska State Fire Marshal / local

stormwater.agency: Nebraska Department of Environment and Energy (NDEE)

building_code.authority: Nevada Department of Business and Industry / local (home-rule)
building_code.enforcement: Primarily local (Las Vegas, Reno strong programs)

stormwater.notes: Arid climate and flash flood considerations

Nevada.notes: Home-rule dominant; major cities have advanced codes.

building_code.authority: New Hampshire Department of Safety / Division of Fire Safety

stormwater.threshold: 1 acre (or 100,000 sq ft outside MS4s in some cases)
stormwater.agency: New Hampshire Department of Environmental Services (DES)

building_code.authority: New Jersey Department of Community Affairs (DCA)

stormwater.threshold: 1 acre or increase in impervious cover ≥0.25 acres
stormwater.agency: New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (DEP)
stormwater.notes: Strong recharge and TSS removal standards

New Jersey.notes: Dense population and strict environmental rules.

building_code.authority: New Mexico Construction Industries Division
New York State Department of State / Division of Building Standards and Codes
building_code.residential: 2020 or 2023 NYS Residential Code (based on 2018/2021 IRC with NY amendments)
building_code.commercial: 2020 or 2023 NYS Building Code (based on 2018/2021 IBC with NY amendments)
building_code.energy: 2020 or later NYS Energy Code (based on IECC with NY stretch options)
building_code.enforcement: State base code with local enforcement (NYC has its own very strict code)

stormwater.agency: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (DEC)
stormwater.notes: Strong post-construction and MS4 requirements; NYC has additional rules

wetlands.notes: Freshwater Wetlands Act + tidal wetlands; very protective

New York.notes: New York City has one of the strictest local codes in the U.S. State code is updated regularly.

building_code.authority: North Carolina Department of Insurance / Engineering Division

stormwater.agency: North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ)

building_code.authority: North Dakota Department of Commerce / local

stormwater.agency: North Dakota Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ)

building_code.authority: Ohio Board of Building Standards
Oklahoma Uniform Building Code Commission / local
Oregon Building Codes Division
building_code.residential: 2021 OR Residential Specialty Code (based on 2021 IRC)
building_code.commercial: 2021 OR Structural Specialty Code (based on 2021 IBC)
building_code.energy: 2021 OR Energy Efficiency Specialty Code (based on IECC with OR amendments)

Oregon.notes: Progressive energy and green building standards.

building_code.authority: Pennsylvania Department of Labor and Industry
Rhode Island State Building Code Standards Committee

stormwater.agency: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management (DEM)

Rhode Island.notes: Leader in adopting 2024 IECC with electric-ready provisions.

building_code.authority: South Carolina Building Codes Council

stormwater.agency: South Carolina Department of Health and Environmental Control (DHEC)

building_code.authority: South Dakota Department of Public Safety / local

stormwater.agency: South Dakota Department of Agriculture and Natural Resources (DANR)

building_code.authority: Tennessee Department of Commerce and Insurance / State Fire Marshal

stormwater.agency: Tennessee Department of Environment and Conservation (TDEC)

building_code.authority: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) / local (strong home-rule)
building_code.enforcement: Primarily local / home-rule (Houston, Austin, Dallas have robust codes)

stormwater.notes: Standard; local MS4 rules may be stricter

Texas.notes: No mandatory statewide building code for most structures. Major cities drive standards. Check specific city.

building_code.authority: Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing / Building Codes
Vermont Department of Public Safety / Division of Fire Safety
Virginia Department of Housing and Community Development (DHCD)

wetlands.notes: Tidal and nontidal wetlands; Bay protections

building_code.authority: Washington State Building Code Council
building_code.residential: 2021 Washington State Residential Code (based on 2021 IRC)
building_code.commercial: 2021 Washington State Building Code (based on 2021 IBC)
building_code.energy: 2021 Washington State Energy Code (based on IECC with WA amendments; very progressive)

Washington.notes: One of the most progressive states for energy and environmental codes.

building_code.authority: West Virginia State Fire Marshal / local

stormwater.agency: West Virginia Department of Environmental Protection (DEP)

building_code.authority: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)
building_code.residential: Uniform Dwelling Code (SPS 320-325) - statewide for 1-2 family since 1980
building_code.commercial: Commercial Building Code (SPS 361-366) - adopts 2021 ICC codes with WI amendments (effective for plans ~Nov 2025)
building_code.energy: SPS 322 (2009 IECC reference for UDC); 2021 IECC for CBC
building_code.enforcement: Statewide code; local municipalities enforce (UDC); DSPS or delegated for commercial plan review

stormwater.notes: NOI required 14 working days prior; Erosion Control Plan + Post-Construction Stormwater Management Plan; weekly inspections; fees by acreage

wetlands.notes: General or Individual permits required for fill/excavation in wetlands or navigable waters; alternatives analysis and mitigation often required

other_key_permits: Air construction permits (NR 406) for new emission sources
Wisconsin.notes: Detailed companion guide available. DSPS eSLA portal for commercial plan review. Enviro-Nav tool for environmental checklist.

building_code.authority: Wyoming Department of Fire Prevention and Electrical Safety / local
